title: Ipomoea cissoides
intro: Ipomoea cissoides, also known as the Grapevine Morning Glory, is a perennial vine native to the Americas, characterized by its climbing habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea cissoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Ipomoea cissoides is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ipomoea cissoides belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. This plant is a species of morning glory. Ipomoea cissoides is native to North America. The classification for Ipomoea cissoides is a flowering plant. It is also known by the common name 'half-hitch' morning glory.
